metadata.title: Mobile-platform performance review sub-rubric
metadata.description: Mobile-specific performance criteria layered atop the universal base perf rubric. Covers cold/warm start, frame rate (60 fps target / jank budget), battery, binary-size budgets (APK <50 MB, IPA <100 MB), network efficiency, and asset optimisation.

severity_rules.pattern: Cold-start time exceeds 2.0 seconds on a mid-tier device (e.g., iPhone SE 2nd-gen, Pixel 4a); pre-main / Application.onCreate work blocks first frame.
severity_rules.description: Both stores rank apps with slow cold start lower in search; users abandon apps that take >3 s to render their first useful frame.
severity_rules.remediation: Defer non-critical initialisation off the launch path; use App Startup library (Android) or pre-main optimisation guides (iOS); measure with the platform tracing tools.

severity_rules.pattern: Warm-start latency exceeds 1.0 s; the app re-runs cold-start initialisation on every foregrounding instead of resuming from the prior state.
severity_rules.description: Warm-start performance is a quality signal in App Store and Play Store rankings and in user reviews.
severity_rules.remediation: Cache initialised singletons across foreground transitions; defer state-restore work to a background queue.

severity_rules.pattern: Scrolling list / animation drops below 60 fps (16.67 ms per frame) or 120 fps on ProMotion devices; jank exceeds the per-frame budget on the main thread.
severity_rules.description: Frame drops manifest as visible jitter that users describe as 'laggy'. The 60 fps budget is the platform contract.
severity_rules.remediation: Profile with Core Animation Instrument / Android GPU Rendering tools; reuse cells; pre-rasterise complex backgrounds; offload computation off the main thread.

severity_rules.pattern: Animations use synchronous CPU-bound work per frame (e.g., complex CALayer rasterisation, repeated bitmap allocation) without leveraging GPU compositing or pre-rendered assets.
severity_rules.description: Per-frame CPU work scales with view complexity and breaks the 60 fps budget on lower-tier devices that the long tail of users still hold.
severity_rules.remediation: Set shouldRasterize on stable layers; use Lottie / AVAnimation for complex motion; profile across the supported device matrix.

severity_rules.pattern: App keeps GPS, Bluetooth scan, or audio session active during background or after the user navigates away; high background CPU usage flagged by the OS.
severity_rules.description: Battery drain is the #1 reason cited in 1-star reviews on both stores. iOS surfaces high-drain apps in Settings → Battery; Android in Battery Manager.
severity_rules.remediation: Release sensors on viewDidDisappear / onPause; subscribe to significant-location-change instead of continuous GPS; throttle BLE scans.

severity_rules.pattern: App polls a server on a short fixed interval (<5 minutes) when the foreground need does not justify the polling rate.
severity_rules.description: Polling on short intervals over cellular drains battery and burns user data; users notice and uninstall.
severity_rules.remediation: Use push notifications for server-initiated updates; on Android, use WorkManager with constraints; on iOS, use Background App Refresh sparingly.

severity_rules.pattern: Final APK exceeds 50 MB or final IPA (universal) exceeds 100 MB; assets, frameworks, or unused code dominate the binary.
severity_rules.description: Above 50 MB APK / 100 MB IPA, install-conversion drops sharply, especially in emerging markets where data and storage are scarce. Above 150 MB on iOS, the user must be on Wi-Fi to install.
severity_rules.remediation: Ship as Android App Bundle / iOS App Thinning; strip debug symbols; remove unused localisations; audit Frameworks/ for duplicate dependencies; compress images to WebP / HEIC.

severity_rules.pattern: Bundled assets (images, fonts, video) ship at 3x / 4x resolution targeting all devices instead of being device-density-resolved at install/runtime.
severity_rules.description: Shipping 3x assets to 1x devices wastes ~10x storage. Both platforms support density-resolved asset delivery — use it.
severity_rules.remediation: Use Asset Catalogs with @1x/@2x/@3x slicing on iOS and `mipmap-*dpi` / split APKs on Android; convert to WebP where supported.

severity_rules.pattern: App makes multiple uncoalesced HTTP calls in a single screen-render path (e.g., one per list item) when a batched endpoint exists or could be requested.
severity_rules.description: Each request carries TCP+TLS overhead (~300 ms on cellular). Uncoalesced calls turn a 2 s render into a 10 s render.
severity_rules.remediation: Coalesce N requests into one batched endpoint; use HTTP/2 multiplexing; cache responses with appropriate Cache-Control headers.

severity_rules.pattern: Image / data downloads do not use HTTP caching (ETag, Cache-Control) and re-download identical assets on every screen visit.
severity_rules.description: Re-downloading already-cached data wastes battery, data, and time. Cache headers exist precisely for this.
severity_rules.remediation: Use URLSession's default cache or SDWebImage / Coil / Glide; respect Cache-Control / ETag from the server.
